name: Suspicious NTDS Process Writes
notes:
This rule detects suspicious processes that write (copy) a Active Directory database (ntds.dit) file.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: NTDS.DIT Creation By Uncommon Process
SIGMA Description: Detects creation of a file named "ntds.dit" (Active Directory Database) by an uncommon process or a process located in a suspicious directory


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created, System.Successful File Modification
when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of ntds.dit

when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES '(cmd|powershell|pwsh|mshta|(w|c)script|wsl).exe' or (LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\appdata\%' or LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\temp\%' or LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\public\%' or LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\perflogs\%') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious NTDS Process Writes on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created, System.Successful File Modification
AND when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of ntds.dit

AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES '(cmd|powershell|pwsh|mshta|(w|c)script|wsl).exe'  or (LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\appdata\%' or LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\temp\%' or LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\public\%' or LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\perflogs\%') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Process Writes Ntds.dit
Event description: Detects suspicious processes that write (copy) a Active Directory database (ntds.dit) file
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
